output.code:
# Let's inspect files in /mnt/data
import os, textwrap, json, re

os.listdir('/mnt/data')

# Read the file content
path = '/mnt/data/file-Xt4qGA9QJd5oRHZ2Pd1UdN-Q077.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:4000]

import re

text = open('/mnt/data/file-Xt4qGA9QJd5oRHZ2Pd1UdN-Q077.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()

# Helper function to classify based on nearby context
def classify_span(start, end, text):
    # look back 50 chars for context
    ctx_start = max(0, start-80)
    ctx_end = min(len(text), end+80)
    ctx = text[ctx_start:ctx_end]
    # tokens that indicate Quran
    if re.search(r'(قوله\s+تعالى|قال\s+تعالى|وقال\s+تعالى)', ctx):
        return 'Aya'
    # bracket with sura: ayah nearby
    if re.search(r'\[[^\[\]]+:\s*[\d٠-٩]+\]', text[end:end+40]):
        return 'Aya'
    # tokens that indicate hadith
    if re.search(r'(حديث|رواه|قال رسول الله|النبي\s+صلى الله عليه وسلم)', ctx):
        return 'Hadeeth'
    return 'Unknown'

# 1) Extract quoted segments between double quotes
quote_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text):
    span_text = m.group(1)
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    typ = classify_span(start,end,text)
    quote_spans.append({'start':start,'end':end,'text':span_text,'type':typ})

quote_spans

# Find all [sura: ayah] bracket markers
brackets = list(re.finditer(r'\[([^\[\]:]+):\s*([\d٠-٩]+)\]', text))
len(brackets), [(m.group(0), m.start(), m.group(1), m.group(2)) for m in brackets[:10]]

import string

# Define boundaries that typically separate sentences or preceding phrases
boundaries = '\n.[]()؛:!؟'  # include Arabic semicolon and question mark

def preceding_phrase_for_bracket(m):
    start_bracket = m.start()
    # Work backwards to find a boundary char
    i = start_bracket - 1
    while i >= 0 and text[i] not in boundaries:
        i -= 1
    phrase_start = i + 1
    phrase = text[phrase_start:start_bracket].strip()
    return phrase_start, start_bracket, phrase

# Extract phrases for bracket occurrences after index 1900 (references region)
results = []
for m in brackets:
    s, e, phrase = preceding_phrase_for_bracket(m)
    results.append({'bracket': m.group(0), 'start': s, 'end': e, 'phrase': phrase, 'bracket_pos': m.start()})
results[:8]

# Extract hadith from "وفي الحديث: " upto period before "رواه"
hadith_spans = []

for m in re.finditer(r'وفي الحديث:\s*([^.\n]+)\.\s*رواه\s+[^\n\.]+', text):
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    hadith_spans.append({'start':start,'end':end,'text':text[start:end]})

hadith_spans[:5], len(hadith_spans)

"وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الدُّنْيَا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الْآخِرَةِ نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا" آل عمران 145
إن هذه تذكرة فمن شاء اتخذ إلى ربه سبيلا الإنسان 29
وقل اعملوا فسيرى الله عملكم ورسوله والمؤمنون التوبة 105

إن أول ما خلق الله القلم فقال له اكتب مقادير الخلق إلى يوم القيامة رواه الترمذي أبو داود

import json, re

text = open('/mnt/data/file-Xt4qGA9QJd5oRHZ2Pd1UdN-Q077.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()
# Pre-identified spans from earlier analysis
quote_spans = [
    {'start': 92, 'end': 143, 'text': text[92:143], 'type':'Aya', 'ref':('الكهف',18,29)},
    {'start': 236, 'end': 304, 'text': text[236:304], 'type':'Aya', 'ref':('الإنسان',76,29)},
    {'start': 418, 'end': 492, 'text': text[418:492], 'type':'Aya', 'ref':('التوبة',9,105)},
    {'start': 632, 'end': 679, 'text': text[632:679], 'type':'Aya', 'ref':('النحل',16,32)},
    {'start': 843, 'end': 899, 'text': text[843:899], 'type':'Hadeeth', 'ref':('Sahih al-Bukhari',8,114)},
    {'start': 1018, 'end': 1085, 'text': text[1018:1085], 'type':'Hadeeth', 'ref':('Jami at-Tirmidhi',47,1)},
]

# Suraqa hadith span
suraqa = {'start': 1318, 'end': 1530, 'text': text[1318:1530], 'type':'Hadeeth', 'ref':('Sahih Muslim',33,2648)}

# Hadith "وفي الحديث: إن الله خير عبدا..." occurrences
hadith_repeats = [
    {'start': 2118, 'end': 2174, 'text': text[2118:2174], 'type':'Hadeeth', 'ref':('Sahih al-Bukhari',8,114)},
    {'start': 2563, 'end': 2619, 'text': text[2563:2619], 'type':'Hadeeth', 'ref':('Sahih al-Bukhari',8,114)},
]

# Bracket-derived references in references region
bracket_spans = [
    {'start':1915, 'end':1993, 'text': text[1915:1993], 'type':'Aya', 'ref':('آل عمران',3,145)},
    {'start':2021, 'end':2091, 'text': text[2021:2091], 'type':'Aya', 'ref':('الإنسان',76,29)},
    {'start':2373, 'end':2440, 'text': text[2373:2440], 'type':'Aya', 'ref':('آل عمران',3,145)},
    {'start':2467, 'end':2537, 'text': text[2467:2537], 'type':'Aya', 'ref':('الإنسان',76,29)},
]

# Now build the citation records
def corrected_text_for(span):
    t = span['text']
    if span['type']=='Aya':
        sura, sura_num, aya_num = span['ref']
        # Provide corrected version for known problematic phrase Al-Imran 145
        if (sura_num, aya_num)==(3,145):
            # Some spans are incomplete; correct to full double-phrase as cited
            return 'وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الدُّنْيَا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الْآخِرَةِ نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا'
        # For others, keep as-is (excerpt exact)
        return t
    else:
        # Hadith corrections
        # For Bukhari "إن الله خير عبدا" ensure "ما عند الله"
        if 'إن الله خير عبدا' in t:
            return 'إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَيَّرَ عَبْدًا بَيْنَ الدُّنْيَا وَبَيْنَ مَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ، فَاخْتَارَ مَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ'
        # For the Pen hadith, use Tirmidhi wording
        if 'إن أول ما خلق الله القلم' in t:
            return 'إِنَّ أَوَّلَ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ الْقَلَمَ فَقَالَ لَهُ اكْتُبْ فَجَرَى بِمَا هُوَ كَائِنٌ إِلَى الْأَبَدِ'
        # For Suraqa hadith, keep as in text or standard phrase
        if 'اعملوا' in t and 'ميسر' in t:
            # Keep as is
            return t.strip()
        return t

def build_item(span):
    item = {
        'span_start': span['start'],
        'span_end': span['end'],
        'span_text': span['text'],
        'citation_type': 'Aya' if span['type']=='Aya' else 'Hadeeth',
        'is_valid': True,
        'aya_reference': {},
        'hadeeth_reference': {},
        'corrected_text': corrected_text_for(span),
        'completely_invalid': False
    }
    if span['type']=='Aya':
        sura, sura_num, aya_num = span['ref']
        item['aya_reference'] = {'sura_name': sura, 'sura_number': sura_num, 'aya_number': aya_num}
    else:
        col, book_num, hadith_num = span['ref']
        item['hadeeth_reference'] = {'collection_name': col, 'book_number': book_num, 'hadith_number': hadith_num}
    return item

citations = []
for s in quote_spans:
    citations.append(build_item(s))
citations.append(build_item(suraqa))
for s in hadith_repeats:
    citations.append(build_item(s))
for s in bracket_spans:
    citations.append(build_item(s))

len(citations)

# Preview the citation items
from pprint import pprint
pprint(citations[:5])
print('...')
pprint(citations[5:10])
print('...')
pprint(citations[10:])

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
